MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and opposing parties Larry Houchins and Valerie Houchins.

RULING: DEFENDANT BRAND ENERGY SERVICES OF CALIFORNIA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Not argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Brand Energy Services of California, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826.) Plaintiff testified that insulation dust would fall from the boards when the scaffolding was dismounted and dismantled and dust would float through the air while employees were walking around the process. (Defendant's Ex. E [Houchins Deposition at 746].) Plaintiff also testified that industrial hygiene sampled the insulation and tested it for its content, including asbestos, in the 1980s and 1990s. (Id. at 693.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

RULING: NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE; AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for order advancing the current trial date with preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(e). Defendants Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc., Air & Liquid Systems Corporation, and Parsons Government Services Inc. filed withdrawals of their joinders to Defendant Brand Energy Services of California Inc.'s opposition. The February 18, 2020 trial date is advanced to November 25, 2019 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is December 4, 2019. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is November 21, 2019. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is November 8, 2019. Expert discovery cut-off date is November 22, 2019. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than September 6, 2019. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
